Availability Table
Availability is generally calculated based on how long a service was unavailable over some period. Assuming no planned downtime,
Table 1-1
indicates how much downtime is permitted to reach a given availability level.
Table 1-1.
Availability table

Using an aggregate unavailability metric (i.e., "
X
% of all operations failed") is more useful than focusing on outage lengths for services that may be partially availableâfor instance, due to having multiple replicas, only some of which are unavailableâand for services whose load varies over the course of a day or week rather than remaining constant.
See Equations
Time-based availability
and
Aggregate availability
in
Embracing Risk
for calculations.
